detailed_analysis: copilot_io.py is a Layer-3 interface adapter implementing the CustomizationTypeIO triple (parse / render / extract_pair_id) for four GitHub Copilot customization surfaces: agents, skills, VS Code user-profile instructions (rules), and prompts (slash commands). The code is functionally sound and secure: no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, no silent exception swallowing, no mutable default arguments, and no no-op production bodies. Parsing is delegated to the shared bounded-YAML helpers in markdown_yaml_metadata_block, so the alias-bomb / pathological-input protection (SEC-C-01) is inherited rather than re-implemented. Every function respects the project's 40-line limit; there is no God Class and no deep nesting. The dominant defects are structural and DRY-related. First, at 489 lines the module is well over the project's hard 300-line file limit (NFR-14 / Clean Code). Second, the four parse functions and the four render functions are near-identical templates that differ only in their field/known-set constants and a handful of per-kind branches; this is textbook duplication that the rest of the codebase has elsewhere factored into shared helpers (resolve_artifact_name, metadata_subset, unknown_metadata_fields) but which here is still copy-pasted four ways. Third, the four extract_pair_id_from_copilot_* functions are pure one-line delegations to extract_pair_id_from_md with no added behaviour (Middle Man). Minor issues include an unused artifact_root parameter on parse_copilot_skill_md, a magic 64-character truncation constant in copilot_skill_slug, and a hardcoded literal slug fallback. The spec contract (architecture §5.1 rules 1-5) appears honored: unknown fields are routed to per_agentic_tool_extra, the 'copilot' name appears only in this adapter layer (permitted), and extract_pair_id cannot raise. NFR-15 secret-policy egress does not apply here because this module handles no env/headers/auth.* fields — those live in the mcp_server_io modules.

findings.evidence: 489-line module spanning four customization surfaces (agents, skills, instructions/rules, prompts/slash_commands), each with its own field-constant set, parse, and render.
findings.problem: The module is 489 lines, 63% over the project's hard 300-line file limit (AGENTS.md §3; NFR-14 Clean Code). It carries four distinct customization-type translation concerns in one file. The other Copilot surfaces (instructions, prompts) are VS Code user-profile concerns, distinct from the Copilot CLI agent/skill concerns called out in the module docstring, so the file mixes two product surfaces.

findings.evidence: Each of the four parse_* functions runs the identical skeleton: split_frontmatter -> dict(prior) or empty_canonical(kind) -> canonical['body'] = body -> resolve name -> copy description -> _set_per_tool_data(only=metadata_subset(...), extra=unknown_metadata_fields(...)) -> _set_pair_id(...). Only the kind string, the *_TOOL_ONLY_FIELDS tuple, and the KNOWN_*_FIELDS set differ.
findings.problem: The same parse algorithm is copy-pasted four times, differing only in constant sets and a few per-kind field copies. This is the duplication US-10 Notes explicitly flags as a defect ('Each parallel set of _add_claude_* / _add_codex_* methods that survives the refactor is a defect') and that AGENTS.md §3 Rule of Three targets. A change to the parse skeleton (e.g. a new shared field) must be edited in four places — Shotgun Surgery risk.

findings.evidence: Each render_* runs: frontmatter_for_render(prior_text) -> set pair_id + name -> set_or_remove_empty_metadata_field(description) -> per-field set_or_remove loop over the *_TOOL_ONLY_FIELDS -> per_agentic_tool_extra passthrough loop guarded by KNOWN_*_FIELDS -> render_markdown_with_metadata_block. Only the constant sets and a couple of extra fields differ.
findings.problem: The render algorithm is duplicated four times. The per_agentic_tool_extra passthrough loop (lines 259-261, 317-319, 383-385, 463-465) is byte-identical save for the KNOWN_*_FIELDS constant. Same Shotgun-Surgery maintenance hazard as CQ-02.

findings.evidence:
def extract_pair_id_from_copilot_agent_md(text: str) -> str | None:
    return extract_pair_id_from_md(text)
findings.problem: Four named functions each do nothing but return extract_pair_id_from_md(text). They add no behaviour, no validation, and no kind-specific logic. They exist only to give the registry four distinct callables; a single shared reference would serve identically. Pure indirection (Fowler Middle Man).

findings.evidence: def parse_copilot_skill_md(text, prior_canonical=None, *, artifact_path=None, artifact_root=None) -> dict[str, Any]:  # artifact_root never referenced in body
findings.problem: artifact_root is declared keyword-only but never used in the function body (only artifact_path is consulted, line 286-287). It is present for callable-signature uniformity across the registry, but as written it is dead surface that misleads a reader into thinking skill naming considers the root the way prompt naming does.

The slug length cap 64 is an inline magic number with no named constant stating its unit/intent (AGENTS.md §9). The fallback string "converted" is also a hardcoded literal with no named constant; both should be module-level named constants (e.g. COPILOT_SKILL_SLUG_MAX_LEN, DEFAULT_SKILL_SLUG).

The local `relative` is overloaded: on one branch it is a Path (relative_to result), on another a str (artifact_path.name), forcing the downstream isinstance(relative, str) type-switch. A name like `relative_path_or_name` plus splitting the two cases would remove the type ambiguity. Minor (AGENTS.md §4 two-word-minimum / single-concept naming).

deviations.description: 489-line module exceeds the project hard 300-line file limit and re-implements the parse/render skeleton four times instead of factoring shared cohesive helpers. Violates the 'small cohesive functions, minimal duplication' clause of NFR-14 / Clean Code.

verdict_summary: Functionally correct and secure adapter, but over the 300-line file limit with the parse/render skeleton duplicated four ways — structural/DRY MAJOR findings, zero CRITICAL.
